key_insight: AI is shifting nanoparticle electron microscopy from image interpretation to scientific inference by extracting structure, dynamics, and properties from TEM, HRTEM, STEM, and in situ datasets.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more quantitative nanoparticle characterization and supports closed-loop materials discovery by linking microscopy, simulations, and synthesis conditions.
method: Conventional ML, CNNs, and transformer models for detection, segmentation, morphology, and defect analysis
Self-supervised, foundation, and multimodal learning for atomic-resolution restoration and 2D-to-3D inference
Physics-informed and autonomous workflows that combine microscopy with simulations, metadata, and in situ experiments
evidence_signal: The review synthesizes applications across multiple microscopy modalities and explicitly assesses benchmarking, data requirements, advantages, and limitations of current AI methods.
watch_item: Performance can be limited by scarce labeled data, weak benchmarking, and the challenge of ensuring physically consistent predictions across heterogeneous experiments.
paper_title: The evolution of AI from image interpretation toward scientific inference in nanoparticle electron microscopy
